title: PutObject, Metadata, Checksums, and Encryption Defaults · Go Semantics Case: Checksum Algorithm Contract
summary: A service uploads an unbounded reader, uses the returned ETag as a universal digest, loses content metadata on replacement, and cannot prove which bytes S3 accepted. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the checksum algorithm contract increment in single-object upload and integrity evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Select supported checksum algorithms, send full-object evidence, validate service response, and retain an application integrity record.
Own and close input bodies, preserve cancellation, bound bytes, and distinguish request completion from later business publication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Checksum Algorithm Contract · Theory Checksum Algorithm Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5908 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove accepted bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without using ETag as a universal digest; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select supported checksum algorithms, send full-object evidence, validate service response, and retain an application integrity record.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Checksum Algorithm Contract contract for the supplied data.
